title: Sleep difficulty
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Supports sleep initiation or maintenance difficulty, early waking, non-restorative sleep, adequate opportunity, fatigue, concentration, mood, irritability, daytime sleepiness, motivation, duration, stress, and functional impact.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports mapped sleep-pattern, duration, stress, mood, daytime-function, and history fields.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports sleep initiation or maintenance difficulty, early waking, non-restorative sleep, adequate opportunity, fatigue, concentration, mood, irritability, daytime sleepiness, motivation, duration, stress, and functional impact.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Insomnia

exact_section.heading: Sections 5–7 — sleep history and daytime consequences

red_flags.text: Lists sleep attacks, apnoea or gasping, unstable cardiopulmonary disease, stroke, injury during sleep, sleepwalking, excessive daytime sleepiness, substance misuse, depression, anxiety, and significant functional impact; supports sleep-diary and targeted evaluation.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped sleep attacks, breathing concerns, substance concern, severe function, evaluation, and escalation fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists sleep attacks, apnoea or gasping, unstable cardiopulmonary disease, stroke, injury during sleep, sleepwalking, excessive daytime sleepiness, substance misuse, depression, anxiety, and significant functional impact; supports sleep-diary and targeted evaluation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Insomnia

red_flags.text: Supports clinician assessment of suicide, self-harm, harm or neglect, protective and support factors, mental status, cognition, insight, substance use, medical contributors, medication history, thyroid testing, and selected blood testing when indicated.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped mental-status and suicide or self-harm risk documentation when depression is assessed as a comorbidity.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports clinician assessment of suicide, self-harm, harm or neglect, protective and support factors, mental status, cognition, insight, substance use, medical contributors, medication history, thyroid testing, and selected blood testing when indicated.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Depression

exact_section.heading: Section 7 — risk, mental-status examination, and selected investigations

red_flags.text: Lists suicidal ideation or attempt, risk of harm or neglect, moderate or severe depression, and psychotic or bipolar features as escalation concerns.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped suicidal, bipolar, and psychotic warning fields when assessed.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists suicidal ideation or attempt, risk of harm or neglect, moderate or severe depression, and psychotic or bipolar features as escalation concerns.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Depression

investigations.text: Supports caffeine, nicotine, medication, substance, sleep-apnoea, restless-legs, pain, withdrawal, thyroid, dyspnoea, mood, anxiety, life-stress, bedtime-worry, environmental, and sleep-habit review plus selected tests when clinically indicated.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped caffeine, medication, substance, thyroid, blood-test, and contributing-condition review.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports caffeine, nicotine, medication, substance, sleep-apnoea, restless-legs, pain, withdrawal, thyroid, dyspnoea, mood, anxiety, life-stress, bedtime-worry, environmental, and sleep-habit review plus selected tests when clinically indicated.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Insomnia

exact_section.heading: Section 7 — substances, medicines, comorbidity, environment, and selected tests

referral.text: Supports clinician-documented discussion of sleep routine or hygiene, lifestyle context, psychological or medication plans, reassessment, questions, monitoring, and referral without autonomous advice or doses.
referral.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-documented sleep routine, lifestyle, medication, questions, worsening review, follow-up, and referral.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented discussion of sleep routine or hygiene, lifestyle context, psychological or medication plans, reassessment, questions, monitoring, and referral without autonomous advice or doses.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Insomnia
